One thing that I really want to make sure that I'm being clear of, of course, I just fucked something up, is that you want to make sure that you're eliminating all the top margin. It's actually one of the biggest things that I see in funnels, ours included that my team turns in, is that that top space is like prime real estate, and you don't want to waste it with just a margin. And so that's where you can have headlines. That's where you can pull up the entire conversion of the page. I can't tell you like, one of the biggest fixes I ever did was I on our funnel, I made the application, basically right under the headline, rather than having a couple, a couple before and afters, and then the application, because most people didn't even know it was. And so just having it just as soon as they so they could see it was the key. Same thing with schedulers. So I've had a scheduler on the thank you page, and I had enough room that I was able to compress the, the fonts and compress the lines enough that I could get it so that you can actually see the blue button that said, pick the time on the scheduler, not just seeing the top of the schedule, but actually seeing the button. And that gave us a significant lift in scheduling rates. So it's lots of little things like this, that when you go back to that funnel math, that really add up and pay huge dividends.
